documented_contract: `public/api.html` claims every endpoint and every schema.
code_evidence: The API page documents 25 paths; `src/router.js` exposes 84 `/v1` route declarations plus `/health` and `/ready`.
risk: Developers and agents cannot use the API reference as the source of truth.
recommended_action: Generate the API reference from a route manifest or downgrade the copy to a curated subset.

documented_contract: API reference omits several public/auth endpoints.
code_evidence: Omitted examples include `/v1/pricing`, `/v1/anon/bootstrap`, `/v1/anon/claim`, `/v1/public/concepts`, `/v1/public/run`, `/v1/public/featured`, `/v1/spec`, `/v1/signout`, and session login/logout.
risk: Critical onboarding and no-key public runtime flows are undocumented or only documented elsewhere.
recommended_action: Add all public/auth endpoints with auth status, abuse limits, request shapes, and response examples.

documented_contract: API reference omits most synthesis, concept, recipe, and bridge routes.
code_evidence: Omitted examples include `/v1/synthesize`, `/v1/synthesize/batch`, `/v1/verify`, `/v1/publish`, `/v1/concepts`, `/v1/recipes`, `/v1/search`, `/v1/compose`, `/v1/bridges/*`, and `/v1/memory/recall`.
risk: The most important product APIs are only partially discoverable from `/api`.
recommended_action: Document the full concept/recipe lifecycle or mark it internal/legacy in a generated manifest.

documented_contract: API reference omits admin, diagnostics, Stripe webhook, public submit, jobs, telemetry, and library routes.
code_evidence: Router exposes `/v1/admin/*`, `/v1/stripe/webhook`, `/v1/public/submit`, `/v1/jobs/:id`, `/v1/telemetry`, and `/v1/library`; many are absent from `/api`.
risk: Operators lack accurate HTTP docs for staff and webhook behavior.
recommended_action: Create public, authenticated, admin, webhook, and internal sections with stability labels.

documented_contract: API reference says every endpoint returns JSON errors shaped `{error, code, request_id}`.
code_evidence: Most code paths return only `{error}` or ad hoc JSON; Stripe webhook returns `text/plain`; no common request_id middleware was found.
risk: SDKs that rely on documented machine error fields will fail or lose traceability.
recommended_action: Standardize error middleware or revise docs to exact route-specific shapes.

documented_contract: API reference says bearer auth on private endpoints and names public endpoints narrowly.
code_evidence: Auth middleware also accepts `X-API-Key`, `Authorization`, cookies, and query-string API keys; public-by-placement and allowlisted routes exceed the docs list.
risk: Developers do not see actual credential precedence or query-key deprecation/security posture.
recommended_action: Document accepted auth mechanisms and planned query-key removal; generate public/protected route manifest.

documented_contract: Docs show a `usage` object with `compiles_30d` and `calls_30d`.
code_evidence: Code returns `used`, `remaining`, pending/billing/Stripe fields, OAuth provider flags, and no `usage` object.
risk: Account dashboard or SDK clients built from docs will parse missing fields.
recommended_action: Update docs or route shape and add a snapshot test for account response.

documented_contract: Docs show a nested `tenant` object, active target plan, quota, seats, billing URL, and billing required flag.
code_evidence: Code returns `{ok, plan: currentPlan, pending_plan, billing_url, billing_required, message}` for paid plans and free-specific fields for downgrades.
risk: Billing clients can show the wrong current plan or expect a tenant object that is not returned.
recommended_action: Document pending-plan semantics and exact response shape, including 503 billing-not-configured.

documented_contract: Docs say cancellation drops to free at the end of the paid period and returns `downgrades_to`/`effective_at`.
code_evidence: Code immediately updates plan to free, clears Stripe subscription id best-effort, and returns `{ok, plan, stripe_cancelled, message}`.
risk: Customers receive different cancellation timing than the API reference promises.
recommended_action: Choose immediate versus period-end cancel semantics and align code, docs, and tests.

documented_contract: Docs require `{confirm}` and return `deleted_at`.
code_evidence: Route does not inspect `confirm`; it soft-deletes tenant and returns `{ok, message}` without `deleted_at`.
risk: API callers can delete without server-side confirmation, and clients expecting `deleted_at` break.
recommended_action: Enforce confirmation server-side or remove it from docs; return stable deletion metadata.

documented_contract: Docs use `cmp_` ids and statuses such as `ready`; code creates `job_` ids and statuses queued/running/completed/failed.
code_evidence: `createJob()` prefixes `job_`; `/v1/compile/:id` adds `artifact_url` when status is completed.
risk: Client polling code generated from docs can use wrong id/status expectations.
recommended_action: Update examples to real `job_` status vocabulary or change code intentionally.

documented_contract: Docs include fields such as `mode` and imply k-sample/frontier orchestration details.
code_evidence: Route accepts `task`, `examples`, `corpus_namespace`, `base_model`, and `deploy_hook`; `mode` is ignored and examples are capped at 200.
risk: Users can send documented fields that do nothing while missing real constraints.
recommended_action: Document accepted fields, limits, serverless sync mode, and deploy hook behavior.

documented_contract: Docs show list rows with `id`, `status: ready`, `size_mb`, and compact K-score values.
code_evidence: Code returns persisted compile job objects with artifact bytes, manifest, k_score, stages, progress, timestamps, and no `size_mb` field.
risk: Dashboards or SDKs built from docs will need undocumented fallback parsing.
recommended_action: Add response snapshots and generate examples from fixtures.

documented_contract: API reference documents only `GET /v1/artifacts` in detail.
code_evidence: Router also exposes `GET /v1/artifacts/:id` and `GET /v1/artifacts/:id/download`; README lists them but `/api` does not.
risk: Artifact retrieval clients must infer detail/download endpoints from README or code.
recommended_action: Add artifact detail/download schemas, errors, and content-type behavior to `/api`.

documented_contract: API reference says `/v1/registry/export` is a bulk NDJSON export.
code_evidence: Code returns a JSON envelope with `spec`, `exported_at`, `registry_hash`, `recipes_n`, `total_bytes`, and `recipes`.
risk: Consumers expecting NDJSON will fail to parse the public registry export.
recommended_action: Change docs to JSON envelope or add an NDJSON format option.

documented_contract: API reference documents 409 signature mismatch and 410 rotated secret errors.
code_evidence: Route returns 200 JSON for invalid signatures/reasons and 503 when the receipt secret is missing; it does not emit 409/410 in the current code path.
risk: Client error handling built from docs will be wrong and tests may miss invalid receipt states.
recommended_action: Document actual success/invalid envelope or change route to status-code semantics intentionally.

documented_contract: Docs show `{verified, reasons, mode}` as the full response.
code_evidence: Legacy `rs-1` receipt verification returns `{verified, valid, checks, receipt, reasons}`; v0.1 returns receipt metadata; no-receipt returns `{verified:false,reasons}`.
risk: SDKs cannot rely on one documented response shape.
recommended_action: Define one receipt-verify schema with union variants and examples for drive-by, v0.1, and legacy receipts.

documented_contract: API docs say every forwarded call records an `(input, output)` pair and says upstream keys are stripped before logging.
code_evidence: Code does not store upstream keys, but stores prompt text and response text in `observations`; errors can also be stored as response strings.
risk: Docs understate raw prompt/response retention and overstate pair-only storage.
recommended_action: Document raw text retention, limits, errors, purge controls, and opt-out/redaction behavior.

documented_contract: Docs show a 200 success response with `distill_` job id and status URL.
code_evidence: Code returns 400 below 1000 captures, 503 when trainer bridge is missing, and only posts to a bridge when configured; no local status route was found for the documented URL.
risk: Preview route examples may look generally available when most environments return 503.
recommended_action: Lead with 503/operator requirements and document bridge response passthrough exactly.

documented_contract: API docs show `/health` liveness with stats and version.
code_evidence: Router also has authenticated `/v1/health` with fuller runtime/readiness detail, but `/api` omits it; README mentions `/v1/health`.
risk: Operators can miss the richer authenticated health snapshot.
recommended_action: Add `/v1/health` alongside `/health` and `/ready`, with auth status and response shape.

documented_contract: `public/docs.html` claims every example is real captured production output from `kolm.ai` on 2026-05-11.
code_evidence: Several examples/response shapes conflict with current local route behavior found in this audit and SDK audit.
risk: A dated live-output claim becomes high-risk unless examples are regenerated from tests.
recommended_action: Generate docs examples from live/local smoke fixtures and store the capture date and commit/deploy id.

documented_contract: Docs show multiple install paths and package names across README, docs, quickstart, and SDK references.
code_evidence: SDK/CLI audit already found root package/private metadata and docs install names are fragmented.
risk: Users can start from API docs but install a package path that does not match the working CLI surface.
recommended_action: Tie docs to one tested quickstart command and run it in CI.

documented_contract: API page metadata says every request schema and response schema exists; the page is hand-authored HTML, not generated OpenAPI.
code_evidence: No OpenAPI file, route manifest, or schema generation path was found in this slice.
risk: Manual docs will keep drifting as routes grow.
recommended_action: Add OpenAPI or JSON route manifest generated from code and use it for SDK/docs/tests.

documented_contract: README lists more endpoints than `/api`, but still omits many routes and does not include request/response schemas.
code_evidence: README lists broad surfaces including admin endpoints, but omits bridge/capture/specialist details and route-level auth boundaries.
risk: Multiple partial API inventories create conflicting source-of-truth problems.
recommended_action: Replace manual inventories with one generated table consumed by README and `/api`.

documented_contract: Root tests check static links and source hygiene, but not docs request/response examples against route snapshots.
code_evidence: `tests/site.test.js` validates links/encoding/inline scripts; no snapshot test parses `/api` examples and compares them to router handlers.
risk: Docs can drift while tests stay green.
recommended_action: Add docs contract tests that execute representative examples or compare generated schema snapshots.
